tasks.label: My share of the group project
Teammate's share (just dumped on me)

memory.summary: Teammate_A is asking me to take over their part of the project today, which conflicts with my plan to finish only my share this afternoon.
I made good progress on my share, but teammate_A is urgently asking for help again despite my current workload.
I learned that setting a reasonable boundary won't damage our trust, and teammate_A is open to splitting or deferring their part.
I made solid progress on my share, reaching 0.59 completion. This confirms that focusing on my own tasks is effective and supports the boundary I set with Teammate_A.
I've reached 70% completion on my share, but with less than an hour left until the deadline, I still have critical work to finish.
Both my 72% progress and the teammate's 0% completion missed the 19:00 deadline, leaving me with a massive backlog despite prior boundary setting.
The deadline has passed with my share at 72% and Teammate_A's at 0%, leaving a critical backlog despite prior progress.
After two hours of work, my share is now at 79%, yet the deadline has passed and my teammate remains at 0%.
Despite working 1.5 hours, my progress only reached 84% while the teammate remains at 0%, leaving a critical gap to close.
My progress has reached 88%, but the deadline passed long ago and my teammate remains at 0%, leaving a critical gap despite my continued effort.
After two more hours of work, my share reached 94%, yet the deadline has passed and my teammate remains at 0%.
My share is nearly complete at 96%, but my teammate remains at 0% despite the deadline passing, leaving the project critically unfinished.
